incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 5 (incorrect - values don't all land exactly on multiples of 5)

params.description: All four values highlighted in data table.

steps.text: Look at the ones place digits: 3, 1, 9, 6. None are 0 or 5. Which numbers can Scale of 5 show exactly?

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 10 (incorrect - values don't land on multiples of 10)

steps.text: Scale of 10 would put 23 between 20 and 30, 31 between 30 and 40. Check the ones digits. Are they 0 or 5?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Ones place digits pulse: 3, 1, 9, 6. None are 0 or 5.

steps.text: Look at the ones place: 3, 1, 9, 6. These aren't 0 or 5, so these numbers aren't multiples of 5. Scale of 5 and Scale of 10 can't show them exactly. Scale of 2 works for any whole number because halfway between each mark is 1. The answer is Scale of 2.
